answer_ref: The system preprocesses images by cropping and resizing objects, creates embeddings using an image encoder, retrieves them via k-NN, and ranks them using MLLMs based on relevance.
prediction: The multimodal retriever system processes and ranks objects for recognition in detection datasets by first encoding the input image into embeddings. These embeddings are then used in a k-nearest neighbors (k-NN) search to retrieve the top-k related images from the memory index. The retrieved top-k categories are then refined and ranked by a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM), which combines the internal knowledge of the model with the retrieved information to make the final prediction of the image category. This process ensures a more accurate and contextually aware classification prediction, enhancing the performance in few-shot and zero-shot perception tasks.
